paper_title: Sampling and Laboratory Methods for Dune Sediments

methods.description: Process for sampling dune sediments by augering to analyze sediment texture, color, grain size, and sorting.

steps.description: The dune sediments were sampled by augering to 5.9 m depth from the eastern crest.
steps.searchable_summary: auger dune sediments to 5.9 m depth

steps.description: Sediment texture and colour, general grain size and sorting, were observed at 0.2 m intervals down the auger hole.
steps.searchable_summary: observe sediment texture, color, grain size, and sorting at 0.2 m intervals
outputs: observations of texture, color, grain size, sorting

steps.description: Four non-oriented samples were taken for thin section microscopy at depths of 0.7 m, 2.9 m, 4.5 m and 5.8 m.
steps.searchable_summary: collect non-oriented samples for thin section microscopy

methods.description: Process for dating sediment samples using Optically Stimulated Luminescence (OSL) to determine the age of sediment deposition.

steps.description: Three samples were taken for OSL dating at depths of 1.7 m, 3.1 m and 4.1 m.
These were sampled and processed in the laboratory using the methods detailed in Fitzsimmons et al. (2007a).
OSL samples were measured using the Single Aliquot Regenerative (SAR) dose protocol of Murray and Wintle (2000, 2003).
steps.searchable_summary: measure OSL samples using SAR protocol

methods.description: Collection and preparation of undisturbed samples for micromorphological analyses and thin section preparation.

steps.description: A Kubeïna tin was used to collect each undisturbed sample for micromorphological analyses and thin section preparation.
steps.searchable_summary: collect undisturbed samples using Kubeïna tin
